text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Georgie.
zh: 我是乔治。

text: Some kids hate eating broccoli.
zh: 有些孩子讨厌吃西兰花。

text: For others it's mushrooms and worst of all, of course, are Brussels sprouts.
zh: 还有些孩子讨厌蘑菇,当然,最糟糕的莫过于抱子甘蓝了。

text: Every parent of young children knows what food they will and won't eat.
zh: 每个有小孩的家长都清楚自己孩子爱吃什么、不爱吃什么。

text: What food did you dislike as a child, Georgie?
zh: 乔治,你小时候不喜欢吃什么食物?

text: Ooh, when I was little I used to absolutely hate nuts and avocados, but now, as an adult, I absolutely love both of those things.
zh: 哦,我小时候特别讨厌坚果和牛油果,但现在长大了,我特别喜欢这两样东西。

text: I'm not surprised, they're delicious.
zh: 我一点也不惊讶,它们确实很好吃。

text: What about you, Neil?
zh: 你呢,尼尔?

text: Did you dislike any foods?
zh: 你小时候讨厌什么食物吗?

text: Do you know what?
zh: 你知道吗?

text: I think I liked most things.
zh: 我觉得我什么都爱吃。

text: Family dinner time can be fun – a chance to reconnect, hear about everyone's day and share a meal.
zh: 家庭晚餐时间可以很愉快——这是重新联络感情、了解彼此一天生活、一起用餐的机会。

text: But when children refuse to eat the food that's been cooked, the The result is often conflict rather than family fun.
zh: 但当孩子拒绝吃做好的饭菜时,结果往往是引发争执,而不是其乐融融。

text: BBC Radio 4 programme The Food Chain asked child nutritionist and mother of two, Charlotte Stirling -Reed, whether her kids eat everything she gives them.
zh: BBC第四台节目《食物链》采访了儿童营养师、两个孩子的母亲夏洛特·斯特林-里德,问她的孩子是否什么都吃。

text: They definitely don't.
zh: 当然不是。

text: We go through multiple periods of ups and downs where their appetite is much lower and then all of a sudden it comes right back.
zh: 我们会经历多次起起伏伏,有时他们食欲很低,然后突然又恢复了。

text: It depends on the weather, what they've been doing that day.
zh: 这取决于天气,也取决于他们那天做了什么。

text: It can depend on their mood.
zh: 这也可能取决于他们的心情。

text: At dinner time, Charlotte's children go through ups and downs – a phrase meaning that a mixture of good and bad things happen to them.
zh: 晚餐时间,夏洛特的孩子们经历着起起伏伏——这个短语指好坏交替发生在某人身上。

text: Sometimes they eat everything happily, other times they don't.
zh: 有时他们高高兴兴地吃光所有东西,有时候却不然。

text: It depends on many things including their day, the weather and their mood.
zh: 这取决于很多因素,包括他们那天的经历、天气以及心情。

text: A person's mood is the way they're feeling at a particular time.
zh: 一个人的心情,是指他们在某个特定时刻的感受。

text: So, how can parents make dinner time something everyone enjoys?
zh: 那么,家长该如何让晚餐时间变成人人都享受的时光呢?

text: In this episode, we'll be hearing from three experts on young children's eating habits and learning some some useful new words and phrases too.
zh: 在这一集里,我们将听取三位专家关于幼儿饮食习惯的看法,并学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember you'll find all the vocabulary from this episode, along with a quiz and worksheet on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本集的所有词汇,还有测验和练习题。

text: But first, of course, Georgie, I have a question for you.
zh: 不过,乔治,我先有个问题要问你。

text: Charlotte Stirling -Reed is a child nutritionist and she has some tips for stress -free family meals.
zh: 夏洛特·斯特林-里德是一位儿童营养师,她对无压力的家庭用餐有一些建议。

text: Which of these do you think she recommends?
zh: 你觉得她会推荐下面哪一项?

text: Is it a make sure your child eats five pieces of fruit and veg a day?
zh: 是a确保孩子每天吃五份水果和蔬菜?

text: a don't pressure your child to eat everything on the plate, or c introduce your child to lots of new flavours quickly.
zh: 是b不要强迫孩子把盘子里的食物吃光,还是c快速让孩子尝试很多新口味?

text: That's quite hard.
zh: 这题挺难的。

text: They all seem like quite good tips.
zh: 这些听起来都是不错的建议。

text: I'm going to go with c introduce your child to lots of new flavours quickly.
zh: 我选c,快速让孩子尝试很多新口味。

text: OK, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 好的,我们稍后在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Dr Anne Fishel is another expert in child nutrition.
zh: 安妮·菲舍尔博士是另一位儿童营养专家。

text: She co -founded the Family Dinner Project, an organisation promoting children's healthy eating in the US.
zh: 她共同创立了“家庭晚餐计划”,这是一个在美国推广儿童健康饮食的组织。

text: Here, Anne explains one reason why meal times can become stressful, to Ruth Alexander for BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 在这里,安妮向BBC世界服务节目《食物链》的主持人露丝·亚历山大解释了用餐时间为何容易变得紧张。

text: Many parents say dinner time can become a battleground.
zh: 许多家长说,晚餐时间可能变成一场“战场”。

text: So why is it that what you're eating can trigger so much tension?
zh: 那么,为什么吃什么会引发这么大的紧张情绪呢?

text: I think for small children, it's one of the few areas that they have control over.
zh: 我认为,对小孩子来说,这是他们少数能够掌控的事情之一。

text: I mean, I mean, parents can cook what they want and serve it when they want, but it's up to kids to decide whether they're going to eat it.
zh: 我是说,父母可以决定做什么、什么时候端上桌,但吃不吃,由孩子自己决定。

text: And for young kids, that's a big deal.
zh: 对小孩子来说,这是件大事。

text: They have some control.
zh: 他们在这方面拥有一定的掌控权。

text: Dinner time often becomes a battleground, a place where argument or conflict happens.
zh: 晚餐时间常常变成“战场”,也就是发生争吵或冲突的地方。

text: Anne thinks that's because it's one of the few areas where children have control.
zh: 安妮认为,这是因为这是孩子为数不多能掌控的领域之一。

text: For kids, controlling what they're willing to eat is a big deal, something of special importance to them.
zh: 对孩子们来说,控制自己愿意吃什么是件大事,是他们特别在意的事情。

text: If experts like Charlotte and Anne struggle at mealtimes, imagine how hard it could be if your child has a food allergy, a condition where the body makes you sick after eating certain foods.
zh: 如果像夏洛特和安妮这样的专家都在用餐时间感到吃力,那么当孩子患有食物过敏——一种吃了某些食物后身体会不适的状况——情况会有多难可想而知。

text: That was the experience of Sharon Wee, a food writer from Singapore, who speaks here with BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 这正是新加坡美食作家沙伦·维的亲身经历,她在BBC世界服务节目《食物链》中讲述了这段经历。

text: My daughter was born with allergies and it was quite a struggle at first.
zh: 我女儿一出生就有过敏问题,一开始真的很艰难。

text: And to be honest with you, we found the winning formula, which was rice and beans.
zh: 老实说,我们找到了屡试不爽的方法,那就是米饭和豆子。

text: She grew up eating rice and beans probably for the first five years of her life.
zh: 她成长过程中,大概头五年都在吃米饭和豆子。

text: Sharon's daughter's allergies restricted what she could eat, making cooking for the whole family a struggle.
zh: 沙伦女儿的过敏限制了她能吃的食物,这让给全家做饭变得很困难。

text: Luckily, Sharon discovered the winning formula, something her daughter could safely eat – rice and beans.
zh: 幸运的是,沙伦找到了那个“屡试不爽的方法”——女儿可以放心食用的米饭和豆子。

text: A winning formula is a method or strategy that produces successful results.
zh: “屡试不爽的方法”指能带来成功结果的方法或策略。

text: So, Georgie, how can parents help their children develop good eating habits?
zh: 那么,乔治,家长该如何帮助孩子养成良好的饮食习惯呢?

text: Well, there are some things that our experts all agree upon.
zh: 其实,我们的专家们在一些方面意见一致。

text: For one, give children a choice, for example by offering small healthy snacks and dips before the meal.
zh: 首先,给孩子选择的余地,比如在正餐前提供一些健康的小零食和蘸酱。

text: And most importantly of all, create a stress -free atmosphere by not pressurising kids to finish everything on their plate.
zh: 最重要的是,不要强迫孩子吃光盘子里的食物,营造一个没有压力的氛围。

text: Right.
zh: 没错。

text: Well, that also helps with your quiz question, Neil.
zh: 这也帮你解答了刚才的测验题,尼尔。

text: So, why not go ahead and reveal the correct answer?
zh: 那不如现在就公布正确答案吧?

text: I asked you what child food nutritionist Charlotte Stirling -Reed recommends for stress -free family meals.
zh: 我之前问你,儿童营养师夏洛特·斯特林-里德为无压力的家庭用餐推荐了什么建议。

text: Yes, I said c introduce your child to lots of new flavours quickly.
zh: 是的,我选了c,快速让孩子尝试很多新口味。

text: I'm afraid you were wrong, Georgie.
zh: 恐怕你答错了,乔治。

text: The answer is actually b don't pressure your child to eat everything on the plate.
zh: 正确答案其实是b,不要强迫孩子把盘子里的食物吃光。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with ups and downs – a phrase describing the mixture of good and bad things that happen to someone.
zh: 好,让我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,先从“起起伏伏”开始——形容一个人经历好坏交替的情况。

text: Your mood is how you feel at a particular time.
zh: “心情”指你在某一特定时刻的感受。

text: A battleground is a place or situation where argument or conflict happens.
zh: “战场”指发生争吵或冲突的地方或情境。

text: If something is a big deal, it's of special importance to you.
zh: 如果某事是“件大事”,意味着它对你来说特别重要。

text: A food allergy is a medical condition where certain foods make your body sick.
zh: “食物过敏”是一种医学状况,指某些食物会让你的身体感到不适。

text: And finally, a winning formula is a method or strategy that produces successful results.
zh: 最后,“屡试不爽的方法”指能带来成功结果的方法或策略。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you'd like to learn more vocabulary on the topic of of food, you can head to our topic page on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,如果你想学习更多关于食物话题的词汇,可以前往我们网站bbclearningenglish.com上的相关主题页面。

text: See you again soon, but for now it's goodbye.
zh: 我们很快再见,不过现在先说再见。

text: 6 Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 《六分钟英语》,来自bbclearningenglish.com
